metadata.subject: Mathematics
metadata.description: Explore the development of mathematical thought across centuries and civilizations, tracing how concepts like number systems, geometry, and algebra evolved. Examine contributions from diverse cultures including African, Asian, Indigenous, and European mathematicians. Discover how First Peoples of BC developed sophisticated mathematical systems for navigation, astronomy, and resource management.
metadata.general_description: A journey through mathematical history examining how diverse cultures shaped modern mathematics.
cross_curricular_connections: Indigenous Studies - First Peoples Knowledge Systems

big_ideas.statement: Mathematics has developed over millennia through the contributions of diverse civilizations and cultures.
big_ideas.elaboration: Different cultures independently developed mathematical concepts to solve practical problems. Understanding this history helps us appreciate mathematics as a human endeavor rather than a fixed body of knowledge.

big_ideas.statement: Number systems and notation evolved from practical counting needs to abstract mathematical representation.
big_ideas.elaboration: From tally marks to place-value systems, the ways humans represent numbers reflect their cultural contexts and problem-solving requirements. Each innovation enabled more complex mathematical operations.

big_ideas.statement: Geometric understanding emerged from natural observation and practical applications in building, land measurement, and astronomy.
big_ideas.elaboration: Geometry developed independently across civilizations as people studied patterns in nature and created structures. Ancient monuments and artifacts demonstrate sophisticated geometric knowledge.

big_ideas.statement: First Peoples of BC developed advanced mathematical systems embedded in cultural practices, art, and resource management.
big_ideas.elaboration: Indigenous mathematical knowledge is reflected in basketry patterns, navigation systems, astronomical observations, and sustainable resource management practices that continue today.

categories.name: Reasoning and Modelling
categories.raw: Develop mathematical thinking by analyzing historical mathematical problems and the reasoning used to solve them across different cultures.

competencies.raw: Analyze and compare mathematical approaches from different cultures to understand diverse problem-solving strategies.
i_can_statements: I can identify the mathematical problems that motivated historical discoveries in different cultures.
I can compare different methods of solving the same mathematical problem across civilizations.
I can recognize patterns in how mathematical ideas developed across time and space.
I can explain the practical contexts that led to specific mathematical innovations.

categories.name: Understanding and Solving
categories.raw: Understand mathematical concepts by exploring their historical origins and development across different civilizations.

competencies.raw: Reconstruct historical mathematical concepts and methods to understand their evolution and modern applications.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate historical mathematical techniques and explain how they relate to modern methods.
I can trace the development of a mathematical concept from its origins to its current form.
I can explain how different number systems work and why they were developed.
I can apply historical mathematical tools and approaches to solve problems.

categories.name: Communicating and Representing
categories.raw: Express understanding of mathematical history through multiple representations and clear explanations of how ideas evolved.

competencies.raw: Communicate mathematical concepts from history using appropriate language, symbols, and cultural context.
i_can_statements: I can explain historical mathematical symbols and notations in their cultural context.
I can create timelines and visual representations showing how mathematical ideas developed.
I can present the contributions of specific mathematicians and cultures to mathematical knowledge.
I can compare historical and modern mathematical notation to explain how representation has evolved.

categories.name: Connecting and Reflecting
categories.raw: Connect historical mathematical knowledge to modern applications and reflect on the cultural significance of mathematical development.

competencies.raw: Reflect on how mathematical history reveals the interconnectedness of cultures and the ongoing evolution of mathematical thinking.
i_can_statements: I can explain connections between historical mathematical developments and contemporary mathematics.
I can analyze how cultural values and worldviews influenced mathematical thinking in different civilizations.
I can recognize the ongoing contributions of diverse cultures to modern mathematics.
I can reflect on how learning mathematical history changes my understanding of mathematics as a discipline.

areas.name: Ancient Number Systems and Notation
areas.raw: Explore how different civilizations developed systems for counting, recording, and calculating numbers.
topics: Tally systems and early counting methods
Egyptian hieroglyphic numerals and the decimal system
Babylonian sexagesimal (base-60) system
Roman numerals and their limitations
Hindu-Arabic numerals and the development of zero
Indigenous counting systems

areas.name: Geometry Through the Ages
areas.raw: Trace the development of geometric understanding from practical applications to abstract mathematical proofs.
topics: Geometric knowledge in ancient monuments and architecture
Euclidean geometry and axiomatic proof
Islamic geometric patterns and tessellations
Indigenous geometric designs in art and basketry
Navigation and spherical geometry
Development of coordinate geometry

areas.name: Algebra and Equation Solving
areas.raw: Follow the evolution of algebraic thinking from practical problem-solving to abstract symbol manipulation.
topics: Early algebraic thinking in Babylonian and Egyptian mathematics
Islamic algebra (al-jabr) and al-Khwarizmi
Chinese mathematical methods and equation solving
Development of algebraic notation in Europe
Cubic and quartic equation solutions
Connections between geometric and algebraic thinking

areas.name: Calculus and Mathematical Analysis
areas.raw: Examine the historical development of calculus and the mathematical thinking that enabled it.
topics: Ancient methods of calculating areas and volumes
Infinitesimal reasoning in Greek mathematics
Newton and Leibniz's independent development of calculus
Rigorous foundations of calculus in the 19th century
Indigenous mathematical observations of change and motion
Modern applications and continuing evolution

areas.name: First Peoples Mathematics of BC
areas.raw: Explore the mathematical knowledge systems of Indigenous peoples of British Columbia.
topics: Astronomical observations and calendars
Navigation using stars, tides, and landmarks
Geometric patterns in woven baskets and cedar art
Sustainable resource management mathematics
Oral traditions and mathematical knowledge transmission
Contemporary Indigenous contributions to mathematics
